sections.title: If the player does not have a ghostspeak amulet equipped
If the player has a ghostspeak amulet equipped

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you and why are you here?

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: Once I was a merchant. I worked hard and became wealthy, very wealthy. I was brave too, storing my possessions in two great chests here, where few would dare to interfere with them.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: But I was not strong enough. One day a witch allied with the pirates of the north; between their strength and her magic, they killed me. The pirates carried one of my chests away to their ship.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: I keep vigil here now, as it interests me to watch adventurers come to raid this little chest, whenever they can find one of my keys.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: My bigger chest was taken north to the pirates' ship - a brave adventurer may like to raid that one instead, if they can handle the perils of the north. I kept more wealth in that one.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: Well done, adventurer. I have two chests, containing my wealth from the days when I was alive. The smaller one is here, and the bigger one was taken far to the north, to the pirates' ship.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: You can use the key to open either of them. The bigger one, in the pirates' ship, contains more wealth than this small one.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: If you want my worldly goods, open my chests with my keys. Adventurers occasionally find my keys while slaying monsters in the Wilderness, on assignment from the witch Krystilia.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: I do not know why Krystilia sends adventurers to find my keys instead of looting the chest herself. Perhaps it amuses her to send people into this land to risk their lives.

lines.speaker: Larran
lines.text: I will be here if you return.
